segments.jp: I wake up at seven o'clock every morning.
segments.zh: 我每天早上七点起床。

segments.jp: First, I brush my teeth and wash my face.
segments.zh: 首先，我刷牙洗脸。

segments.jp: Then I eat breakfast with my family.
segments.zh: 然后和家人一起吃早餐。

segments.jp: I usually have bread, eggs, and a glass of milk.
segments.zh: 我通常吃面包、鸡蛋，喝一杯牛奶。

segments.jp: After breakfast, I take the bus to school.
segments.zh: 早餐后，我坐公交去学校。

segments.jp: The bus ride takes about twenty minutes.
segments.zh: 坐公交大约需要二十分钟。

segments.jp: I arrive at school before eight thirty.
segments.zh: 我在八点半之前到达学校。

segments.jp: I like mornings because everything feels fresh and new.
segments.zh: 我喜欢早晨，因为一切都感觉清新而崭新。

segments.jp: There are four people in my family.
segments.zh: 我家有四口人。

segments.jp: My father is a teacher. He teaches math.
segments.zh: 我爸爸是老师，他教数学。

segments.jp: My mother works at a hospital. She is a nurse.
segments.zh: 我妈妈在医院工作，她是护士。

segments.jp: I have one brother. His name is Tom.
segments.zh: 我有一个哥哥，他叫汤姆。

segments.jp: Tom is older than me. He is in college.
segments.zh: 汤姆比我大，他在上大学。

segments.jp: We have a pet dog named Lucky.
segments.zh: 我们有一只宠物狗叫幸运。

segments.jp: On weekends, we often go to the park together.
segments.zh: 周末我们经常一起去公园。

segments.jp: I love my family very much.
segments.zh: 我非常爱我的家人。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Good evening. A table for two, please.
lines.zh: 晚上好，两位，谢谢。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Sure, right this way. Here's the menu.
lines.zh: 好的，这边请。这是菜单。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Thank you. What do you recommend?
lines.zh: 谢谢。你推荐什么？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Our steak is very popular today.
lines.zh: 我们的牛排今天很受欢迎。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: I'll have the steak, please. And a salad.
lines.zh: 请给我来份牛排，再加一份沙拉。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Would you like something to drink?
lines.zh: 您要喝点什么吗？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Just water, please.
lines.zh: 请来杯水就好。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Great. Your order will be ready in fifteen minutes.
lines.zh: 好的，您的餐大约十五分钟就好。

segments.jp: Last summer, I traveled to London for the first time.
segments.zh: 去年夏天，我第一次去了伦敦。

segments.jp: The flight from Beijing took about eleven hours.
segments.zh: 从北京飞过去大约需要十一个小时。

segments.jp: When I arrived, I was surprised by how green the city was.
segments.zh: 到达时，我惊讶于这座城市多么绿意盎然。

segments.jp: I visited Big Ben and the Tower of London on the first day.
segments.zh: 第一天我参观了大本钟和伦敦塔。

segments.jp: The British Museum was my favorite place.
segments.zh: 大英博物馆是我最喜欢的地方。

segments.jp: It has so many things from all over the world.
segments.zh: 那里有来自世界各地的很多展品。

segments.jp: I tried fish and chips, which is a famous British dish.
segments.zh: 我尝了炸鱼薯条，这是著名的英国菜。

segments.jp: The weather was cloudy most days, but I didn't mind.
segments.zh: 大多数时候天气阴沉，但我不介意。

segments.jp: I took the Underground to get around the city.
segments.zh: 我坐地铁在城市里穿梭。

segments.jp: I hope to visit London again someday.
segments.zh: 我希望有一天能再去伦敦。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Please have a seat. Tell me about yourself.
lines.zh: 请坐。介绍一下你自己。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Thank you. I graduated from Beijing University last year.
lines.zh: 谢谢。我去年从北京大学毕业。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: I studied business management for four years.
lines.zh: 我学了四年的工商管理。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Do you have any work experience?
lines.zh: 你有工作经验吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Yes, I worked as an intern at a tech company last summer.
lines.zh: 有的，去年夏天我在一家科技公司实习过。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: What are your strengths?
lines.zh: 你的优势是什么？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: I'm a fast learner and I work well in a team.
lines.zh: 我学东西很快，而且善于团队合作。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Very good. We'll get back to you by next Friday.
lines.zh: 很好。我们会在下周五前回复你。

segments.jp: Social media has become a huge part of our daily lives.
segments.zh: 社交媒体已经成为我们日常生活的重要组成部分。

segments.jp: Most people check their phones first thing in the morning.
segments.zh: 大多数人早上第一件事就是看手机。

segments.jp: Apps like Instagram and TikTok are especially popular among young people.
segments.zh: Instagram 和 TikTok 等应用在年轻人中特别受欢迎。

segments.jp: On one hand, social media helps us stay connected with friends.
segments.zh: 一方面，社交媒体帮助我们与朋友保持联系。

segments.jp: We can share photos, videos, and messages instantly.
segments.zh: 我们可以即时分享照片、视频和消息。

segments.jp: On the other hand, spending too much time online can be harmful.
segments.zh: 另一方面，在网上花太多时间可能是有害的。

segments.jp: Some studies show that it can increase anxiety and loneliness.
segments.zh: 一些研究表明它可能增加焦虑和孤独感。

segments.jp: Many people compare themselves to others and feel unhappy.
segments.zh: 许多人与他人比较，感到不开心。

segments.jp: It is important to find a healthy balance.
segments.zh: 找到一个健康的平衡很重要。

segments.jp: Try to limit screen time and enjoy real-life moments.
segments.zh: 试着限制屏幕时间，享受现实生活的时刻。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Hi, I'd like to return this jacket, please.
lines.zh: 你好，我想退这件夹克。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Of course. May I ask what the problem is?
lines.zh: 当然可以。请问是什么问题？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: The zipper is broken. I just bought it three days ago.
lines.zh: 拉链坏了。我三天前刚买的。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Do you have the receipt?
lines.zh: 您有收据吗？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Yes, here it is.
lines.zh: 有的，在这里。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Would you prefer a refund or an exchange?
lines.zh: 您想退款还是换货？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: I'd like to exchange it for the same one, if possible.
lines.zh: 如果可以的话，我想换一件同款的。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Let me check if we have it in stock. One moment, please.
lines.zh: 让我查一下是否有库存。请稍等。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Sure, take your time.
lines.zh: 好的，不急。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Good news! We have one left. Here you go.
lines.zh: 好消息！还剩一件。给您。

segments.jp: Climate change is one of the greatest challenges facing humanity today.
segments.zh: 气候变化是当今人类面临的最大挑战之一。

segments.jp: The Earth's average temperature has risen by about 1.1 degrees Celsius since the late 1800s.
segments.zh: 自19世纪末以来，地球的平均温度已上升了约1.1摄氏度。

segments.jp: This warming is mainly caused by burning fossil fuels like coal, oil, and gas.
segments.zh: 这种变暖主要是由燃烧煤炭、石油和天然气等化石燃料引起的。

segments.jp: These fuels release carbon dioxide and other greenhouse gases into the atmosphere.
segments.zh: 这些燃料向大气中释放二氧化碳和其他温室气体。

segments.jp: The effects of climate change are already visible around the world.
segments.zh: 气候变化的影响在世界各地已经显而易见。

segments.jp: Sea levels are rising, glaciers are melting, and extreme weather events are becoming more frequent.
segments.zh: 海平面在上升，冰川在融化，极端天气事件变得更加频繁。

segments.jp: Scientists agree that we must reduce emissions dramatically to avoid catastrophic consequences.
segments.zh: 科学家们一致认为，我们必须大幅减少排放以避免灾难性后果。

segments.jp: Renewable energy sources such as solar and wind power offer a path forward.
segments.zh: 太阳能和风能等可再生能源提供了一条前进的道路。

segments.jp: Individual actions matter too — using public transport, reducing waste, and eating less meat all help.
segments.zh: 个人行动也很重要——使用公共交通、减少浪费和少吃肉都有帮助。

segments.jp: The time to act is now, before the damage becomes irreversible.
segments.zh: 行动的时间就是现在，在损害变得不可逆转之前。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Good morning. What seems to be the problem?
lines.zh: 早上好。哪里不舒服？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: I've had a persistent headache for the past week.
lines.zh: 过去一周我一直头疼。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: I've also been feeling dizzy, especially in the mornings.
lines.zh: 我还一直感到头晕，特别是早上。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Have you been under a lot of stress recently?
lines.zh: 你最近压力大吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Yes, I've been working overtime almost every day.
lines.zh: 是的，我几乎每天都在加班。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: That could be a contributing factor. How's your sleep?
lines.zh: 这可能是一个因素。你睡眠怎么样？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Not great. I usually only get about five hours a night.
lines.zh: 不太好。我通常每晚只睡大约五个小时。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: I'd recommend getting at least seven hours. Let me check your blood pressure.
lines.zh: 我建议你至少睡七个小时。让我量一下你的血压。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Your blood pressure is a bit high. I'll prescribe some medication.
lines.zh: 你的血压有点高。我给你开些药。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Thank you, doctor. Should I come back for a follow-up?
lines.zh: 谢谢医生。我需要来复诊吗？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Hello, I have a reservation under the name Li Wei.
lines.zh: 你好，我预订了房间，名字是李伟。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Let me check. Yes, a single room for three nights.
lines.zh: 让我查一下。是的，单人间，三晚。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: That's correct. Is breakfast included?
lines.zh: 没错。包含早餐吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Yes, breakfast is served from 7 to 10 in the morning.
lines.zh: 是的，早餐供应时间是早上7点到10点。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Great. What floor is my room on?
lines.zh: 好的。我的房间在几楼？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Room 508, on the fifth floor. Here's your key card.
lines.zh: 508房，在五楼。这是您的房卡。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Is there Wi-Fi in the room?
lines.zh: 房间里有Wi-Fi吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Yes, the password is on the card. Enjoy your stay!
lines.zh: 有的，密码在卡上。祝您入住愉快！

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Excuse me, could you tell me how to get to the train station?
lines.zh: 打扰一下，请问怎么去火车站？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Sure. Go straight ahead for two blocks.
lines.zh: 好的。一直往前走两个路口。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Then turn left at the traffic light.
lines.zh: 然后在红绿灯那里左转。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Turn left at the traffic light. Got it.
lines.zh: 在红绿灯左转。明白了。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: You'll see the station on your right. It's about a five-minute walk.
lines.zh: 你会看到车站在右手边。大约走五分钟。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Is it far from here?
lines.zh: 离这里远吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: No, not at all. You can't miss it.
lines.zh: 不远，一点都不远。你不会错过的。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Thank you so much for your help!
lines.zh: 非常感谢你的帮助！

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Hi, I have a bad cold. Can you recommend something?
lines.zh: 你好，我感冒很严重。能推荐点什么吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Do you have a fever or just a runny nose?
lines.zh: 你发烧了还是只是流鼻涕？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: I have a sore throat and a bit of a cough.
lines.zh: 我嗓子疼，还有点咳嗽。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: I'd suggest these throat lozenges and this cough syrup.
lines.zh: 我建议用这些润喉糖和这个止咳糖浆。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: How often should I take the cough syrup?
lines.zh: 止咳糖浆多久吃一次？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Three times a day, after meals.
lines.zh: 一天三次，饭后服用。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Are there any side effects?
lines.zh: 有什么副作用吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: It may cause some drowsiness. Avoid driving after taking it.
lines.zh: 可能会引起一些困倦。服用后不要开车。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Okay, I'll take both. How much is it?
lines.zh: 好的，两样都要。多少钱？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: That'll be twelve pounds fifty. Get well soon!
lines.zh: 一共12.5英镑。祝早日康复！

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Good morning. Can I see your passport and booking confirmation?
lines.zh: 早上好。请出示您的护照和预订确认。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Here you go.
lines.zh: 给你。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Would you like a window seat or an aisle seat?
lines.zh: 您想要靠窗还是靠过道的座位？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: A window seat, please.
lines.zh: 靠窗的，谢谢。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: How many bags are you checking in?
lines.zh: 您要托运几件行李？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Just one suitcase.
lines.zh: 只有一个箱子。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Please place it on the scale. It's within the weight limit.
lines.zh: 请放到秤上。没有超重。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Which gate do I go to?
lines.zh: 我该去哪个登机口？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Gate 23. Boarding starts at 10:45. Have a pleasant flight!
lines.zh: 23号登机口。10:45开始登机。祝您旅途愉快！

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Thank you very much!
lines.zh: 非常感谢！

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Welcome! Let me show you around the apartment.
lines.zh: 欢迎！让我带您看看这间公寓。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: It looks nice. How big is it?
lines.zh: 看起来不错。有多大？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: It's about 60 square meters. One bedroom, one living room.
lines.zh: 大约60平米。一室一厅。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Is the kitchen fully equipped?
lines.zh: 厨房设备齐全吗？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Yes, there's a fridge, an oven, and a washing machine.
lines.zh: 是的，有冰箱、烤箱和洗衣机。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: What's the monthly rent?
lines.zh: 月租多少？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: It's 800 pounds per month, utilities not included.
lines.zh: 每月800英镑，不含水电费。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Is there a minimum lease period?
lines.zh: 有最短租期吗？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Yes, the minimum is six months. You'll also need a deposit.
lines.zh: 有的，最短六个月。还需要交押金。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: I'm interested. Can I think about it and get back to you tomorrow?
lines.zh: 我有兴趣。我能考虑一下明天回复您吗？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Good afternoon. How can I help you today?
lines.zh: 下午好。请问需要什么帮助？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: I'd like to open a current account, please.
lines.zh: 我想开一个活期账户。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Sure. Do you have any identification with you?
lines.zh: 好的。您带身份证件了吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Yes, here's my passport and a proof of address.
lines.zh: 有的，这是我的护照和地址证明。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Would you like to set up online banking as well?
lines.zh: 您也想开通网上银行吗？

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Yes, that would be very convenient.
lines.zh: 好的，那会很方便。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: You'll receive your debit card within five working days.
lines.zh: 您将在五个工作日内收到借记卡。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Can I transfer money internationally with this account?
lines.zh: 用这个账户可以国际转账吗？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Yes, but there's a small fee for international transfers.
lines.zh: 可以，但国际转账有少量手续费。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: That's fine. Let's go ahead and set it up.
lines.zh: 没关系。那就办吧。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Let's get started. The deadline for the project has been moved up to next month.
lines.zh: 我们开始吧。项目截止日期已经提前到下个月了。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: That's quite tight. Do we have enough resources to handle it?
lines.zh: 时间很紧。我们有足够的资源来应对吗？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: I've requested two additional team members from the design department.
lines.zh: 我已经向设计部门申请了两名额外的团队成员。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: What about the budget? Will we need to cut any features?
lines.zh: 预算呢？我们需要砍掉一些功能吗？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: The client wants to keep all core features. We might simplify the reporting module.
lines.zh: 客户希望保留所有核心功能。我们可能会简化报告模块。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: I think that's reasonable. The reporting module can be enhanced in phase two.
lines.zh: 我觉得这很合理。报告模块可以在第二阶段再增强。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Exactly. Let's prioritize the user-facing features first.
lines.zh: 没错。我们先优先处理面向用户的功能。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Should we switch to daily stand-ups to track progress more closely?
lines.zh: 我们是否应该改为每日站会来更紧密地跟踪进度？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Good idea. Let's start that from tomorrow.
lines.zh: 好主意。我们从明天开始。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: I'll send out a revised timeline by end of day.
lines.zh: 我会在今天下班前发出修改后的时间表。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: I believe university education should be free for everyone.
lines.zh: 我认为大学教育应该对所有人免费。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: That sounds ideal, but who would pay for it?
lines.zh: 听起来很理想，但谁来买单？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: The government could fund it through higher taxes on the wealthy.
lines.zh: 政府可以通过对富人加税来资助。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: But wouldn't that discourage investment and economic growth?
lines.zh: 但那不会抑制投资和经济增长吗？

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: Not necessarily. An educated population actually drives innovation and productivity.
lines.zh: 不一定。受过教育的人口实际上推动了创新和生产力。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: That's a fair point. But some countries with free tuition still have quality issues.
lines.zh: 这倒是。但有些免学费的国家仍然存在质量问题。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: True, funding alone isn't enough. You need proper management and accountability.
lines.zh: 确实，光有资金还不够。还需要合理的管理和问责。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Maybe a middle ground would work — reduced fees with scholarships for those in need.
lines.zh: 也许折中方案可行——降低学费，同时为有需要的人提供奖学金。

lines.speaker: A
lines.jp: I could live with that. Equal access to education is what matters most.
lines.zh: 我可以接受。平等的教育机会才是最重要的。

lines.speaker: B
lines.jp: Agreed. Everyone deserves a fair chance, regardless of their background.
lines.zh: 同意。每个人都应该有公平的机会，不论出身。
